questions.question: Explain the difference between int[] arr and int arr[] in Java. Which is preferred and why?
keyPoints: Both declarations are valid in Java
int[] arr is preferred style as it clearly indicates 'arr' is an array of integers
int arr[] is C-style declaration
The [] can be placed either before or after variable name
Preferred style improves code readability

questions.question: What are the default values assigned to array elements when an array is created using 'new' in Java?
keyPoints: Numeric types: 0
Boolean: false
Character: '\u0000' (null character)
Object references: null
This applies to all elements regardless of data type

questions.question: Write a Java program to find the sum of all elements in an integer array.
keyPoints: Use a for loop or enhanced for loop
Iterate through each element
Add each element to a sum variable
Initialize sum to 0 before loop
Print the final sum

questions.question: What is an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException? When does it occur?
keyPoints: Runtime exception in Java
Occurs when accessing array with invalid index
Index is negative OR index >= array length
Arrays are zero-indexed
Should use proper bounds checking to avoid this exception

questions.question: Explain the concept of a Jagged array with an example.
keyPoints: Jagged array is a 2D array with variable row lengths
Each row can have different number of columns
Example: int[][] jagged = new int[3][];
jagged[0] = new int[2]; jagged[1] = new int[4];
Useful when data doesn't form a perfect rectangle

questions.question: What are the advantages and disadvantages of arrays in Java?
keyPoints: Advantages: Fast access O(1), memory efficient, simple syntax
Disadvantages: Fixed size (cannot grow/shrink), no built-in bounds checking
All elements must be same type
Cannot store primitives directly in collections like ArrayList

questions.question: How do you copy an array in Java? Explain at least two methods.
keyPoints: Using Arrays.copyOf() - creates new array with specified length
Using System.arraycopy() - copies elements between arrays
Using clone() method - creates shallow copy
Manual copying using loops
Each method has different use cases and performance
